step_by_step_thinking:
During life, the dead region of myocardium would appear pale due to lack of perfusion during an infarction. At autopsy, tetrazolium chloride staining helps visualize the viability of tissue. Dead cells do not take up the stain and thus remain unstained and pale while live cells do take up the stain and turn brick-red. Therefore, the stained myocardial tissue will exhibit a pale appearance rather than being colored.
\boxed{\text{C}}
